stem: When a user is on a network, is this a situation that would prevent Desktop Single Sign-On (SSO) from signing the user in automatically?

options.text: The IIS Server is not available
The user does not have a valid Kerberos ticket

stem: An Okta Administrator needs to enable Active Directory mastered users to change passwords in Okta. Is this a step that the administrator needs to take?

options.text: Configure Desktop SSO
Set the Active Directory recovery options to true
Install the Okta Password Sync Agent
Ensure the Okta service account has elevated permissions in Active Directory

stem: Is this the default date range for which events are displayed in the Okta system log?

options.text: Thirty days
Seven days

stem: Users are unable to authenticate to Microsoft OneNote when using Desktop SSO/IWA. Is this a likely cause of the issue the users are experiencing?

options.text: Desktop SSO/IWA does NOT work with OneNote
Desktop SSO/IWA was NOT deployed in HTTPS mode

stem: Is this mapping type available when an administrator maps Okta attributes to Active Directory attributes?

options.text: Clone user in Active Directory
Remove duplicate
Delete user in Active Directory
Do not map
Apply mapping on user create only

stem: Is this information contained in a SAML assertion?

options.text: Access token
Audience URI
Entity ID/Issuer ID
IdP authentication assertion

stem:
Last week, an Okta administrator configured Okta to use Active Directory (AD) for Delegated Authentication. This week, a new Active Directory user is unable to log on to Okta.
Is this a reason why the new Active Directory user might be unable to authenticate?

options.text: The user is outside of an Active Directory Organizational Unit (OU) that has been imported into Okta.
The user was not successfully imported into Okta from Active Directory.
The user account is disabled, or the user is locked out.

stem:
An Okta Administrator is creating a custom attribute in Okta named "Initials" to streamline employees' experience with DocuSign. The Initials field must be populated when data is mapped from Active Directory to Okta.
Is this the Okta Expression Language function the administrator should use to complete the task?

options.text: String.replaceFirst
String.join
String.subStringAfter
String.substring

stem: Is this an example of a provisioning operation that Okta can perform in an application?

options.text: Create user
Delete all user passwords in an applciation
Deactivate user
Import a password from an application
Push a password
Update user

stem:
A company has the following environment:
  • All employees and their group memberships are in an Active Directory domain.
  • A Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) server contains all of the company's external users (customers and partners).
  • All of the employee user accounts are replicated in the LDAP server from Active Directory.
  • All company employees, customers, and partners need access to certain applications through Okta.
The company has integrated both Active Directory and LDAP with Okta.
Is this a valid method to resolve the user duplication arising from the employee accounts existing in both Active Directory and LDAP?

options.text: If the employee, customer, and partner accounts are stored in different Organizational Units (OUs) in the LDAP server, then Okta can be configured to exclude the employee OU. This action will prevent the collision with the same users also being imported from Active Directory.

stem: Is this statement true about applications that are available in the Okta Integration Network (OIN)?

options.text: Only Security Assertion Markup Language (SAML)-enabled OIN applications support provisioning
OIN applications are available to all Okta customers
All OIN applications have native mobile apps

stem: Is this what is required to deploy RSA SecureID for high availability with Okta?

options.text: Okta RADIUS Agent
Okta RSA Agent with the configuration ssIPinningEnabled = false
2 On-Prem MFA Agents

stem:
An Active Directory mastered user signs into Okta org with Just-In-Time (JIT) provisioning enabled for the first time. However, the end user's Active Directory group memberships are NOT carried over into Okta.
Is this a possible reason why the groups did not import?

options.text: Group push was NOT enabled.
The groups and the user are in the same domain.
The end user was activated manually.
The groups and the user are in different domains.

stem:
An Okta Administrator has configured a human resource (HR) application to profile master Okta users. The HR application is set up as the highest priority profile master for a group of end users. Reactivate deactivated users is enabled.
Is this the expected behavior when a user is deactivated in the HR application?

options.text: The user loses accesss to the application but maintains access to Okta and all other applciations.
The user account is suspended in Okta and all downstream applications immediately.
The user account is deactivated in Okta

stem: Is this a configurable option in an Okta sign-on policy?

options.text: Allow or deny access
Enforce the enrollment for multifactor authentication (MFA)
Set the password length requirement
Prompt for multifactor authentication (MFA) challenge
Set the length of the security question answer

stem:
A company has configured Okta with Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) Directory. An application the company uses requires an LDAP managerId attribute.
Is this statement about LDAP profile mappings true?

options.text: Any attributes can be mapped to the application profile, as long as they are imported into Okta.
LDAP attributes can only be mapped through Okta API.

stem: Is this one of the permissions that is required when provisioning groups from Okta to Active Directory?

options.text: msDS-ResultantPSO permission for group objects on the target organizational unit (OU)
Domain Admin permission for group objects on the target organizational unit (OU)
Create child permission for group objects on the target organizational unit (OU)

stem:
A custom application is calling Okta Management APIs.
Is this the way the application can remain within the rate limits?

options.text: By using an API gateway between the application and Okta
By using the Introspect API endpoint
By counting every API call from the application
By using the Okta rate limiting headers

stem:
An Okta user needs to approve a request for access to an application that requires the assignment of a license.
Is this the entitlement option that will enable the approver to assign the license?

options.text: Hidden
Read
Update
Write

stem: Is this statement true about Universal Directory mappings?

options.text: Universal Directory supports Java regular expression for transforming data attributes in the mappings
Universal Directory can map attributes both ways from Okta to applications and from applications to Okta, if supported by the application
Universal Directory supports Spring Expression Language (SpEL) for transforming data attributes in the mappings
Universal Directory can only map attributes from Okta to applications

stem: Is this a true statement regarding the Single Sign-On (SSO) URL for a SAML application when an administrator uses the Okta Application Integration Wizard (AIW)?

options.text: The SSO URL represents a user's explicit permission to allow an application to access resources protected by scopes.
The SSO URL provides SSO for applications that do NOT support proprietary federated sign-on methods.
The SSO URL is where the SAML assertion is sent with an HTTP POST.

stem:
A developer needs to build an internal application that uses Okta for authentication and authorization. The developer has asked the Okta Administrator to create an OAuth 2.0 server in Okta.
Is this one of the tasks the administrator must perform in the Okta Administrator console?

options.text: Create an OpenID Connect (OIDC) application
Create a basic template authentication application
Create an authorization server

stem: Is this an example of what a group in Okta can be used for?

options.text: To assign specific entitlements within an application

stem: Is this the purpose of rate limiting?

options.text: Creating API endpoint throttling limits to protect the LDAP service from load spikes or service interruptions caused by submitted requests
Creating API endpoint throttling limits to protect the AD service from load spikes or service interruptions caused by submitted requests
Creating API endpoint throttling limits to protect the Okta service from load spikes or service interruptions caused by submitted requests

stem: Is this the purpose of an MFA enrollment policy?

options.text: Controls how end users interact with an application
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because an MFA enrollment policy controls how end users enroll in an authenticator.

options.text: Determines the types of authentication challenges end users experience when they sign into their account
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the global session determines the types of authentication challenges end users experience when they sign into their account.

options.text: Determines which authenticators are required for administrators
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because an MFA enrollment policy controls how end users enroll in an authenticator.

options.text: Controls how end users enroll in an authenticator
options.explanation: This option is correct

stem: Is this an example of an authenticator method with Device Bound and Hardware Protected characteristics?

options.text: Email
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the email authenticator is a method that has the User Presence characteristic, but NOT the Device Bound or Hardware Protected characteristics.

options.text: Google Authenticator
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the Google Authenticator is a method that has the Device Bound characteristic, but NOT the Hardware Protected characteristics

stem: Is this a configurable password setting in Okta?

options.text: Common password check
Minimum length
Number Challenge
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because Number Challenge is a configurable Okta Verify factor setting, NOT a configurable password setting

stem:
A company recently allowed 100% work-from-home for all employees. The Chief Security Officer wants to ensure user step-up authentication is enforced if an employee uses a new device, does impossible travel, or logs in from a blacklisted IP range.
Is this how an Okta Administrator can implement these requirements?

options.text: By creating or modifying global session policies with the required behavior detected options.
By creating or modifying a group rule with the required behavior detection options.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because group rules CANNOT address the required behavior detection options.

stem: Is this a true statement about Active Directory (AD) groups?

options.text: AD groups are created in AD and imported to Okta; all AD group changes must be done in Okta and pulled into AD.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because AD group changes must be done in AD and pulled into Okta.

options.text: AD groups are created in a downstream application and managed in the application
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because AD groups are not created in downstream applications.

options.text: AD groups are created in AD and imported to Okta; all AD group changes must be done in AD and pulled into Okta.

stem: Is this an appropriate use case for attribute matching?

options.text: Determine what groups a user should be added to based on a particular value of an attribute
options.explanation: Incorrect because group memberships based on a user's attributes is handled through Group Rules.

options.text: Ensure that there is NOT more than one attribute with the same name
options.explanation: Incorrect because attribute matching is a feature of profile sources and enforcement of different attribute names would be done by Universal Directory.

options.text: Ensure all users with a specified attribute are assigned to the same applications
options.explanation: Incorrect because attribute matching is NOT used to assign users to applications.

options.text: Determine whether an imported user is new or if the user profile already exists in Okta

stem:
An Okta Administrator wants to find successful Okta logins for a specific user.
Is this the System Log event that the administrator should query?

options.text: eventType eq "user.authentication.sso.success"
options.explanation: Incorrect because this query is used to search for Application Access events.

options.text: user.account.lock
options.explanation: Incorrect because this query is used to search for User Locked Out events.

options.text: user.authentication.success
options.explanation: Incorrect because this query is not the standard event for primary Okta sign-in.

options.text: user.session.start

stem:
In an Okta org, Okta sourced users are configured with Active Directory (AD). An Okta Administrator needs to push passwords to AD.
Is this a configuration the administrator should perform to enable password synchronization?

options.text: Configure Agentless Desktop Single Sign-on (DSSO)
options.explanation: Incorrect because DSSO is used for delegated authentication when Active Directory (AD) performs primary authentication. In this scenario, users are Okta-sourced.

options.text: Enable Delegated Authentication to Active Directory, if it is disabled
options.explanation: Incorrect because Delegated Authentication needs to be disabled for password synchronization from Okta to AD.

options.text: Install the Okta AD Password Sync Agent
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the Okta AD Password Sync Agent is used to sync the AD password to Okta.

options.text: Disable Delegated Authentication to Active Directory, if it is enabled

stem: An Okta Administrator is trying to integrate Okta Verify with the company's Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) product. Is this a pre-requisite condition for a target device in this situation?

options.text: A macOS device that is registered but not managed
options.explanation: Incorrect because to be enrolled in Okta Verify targeted devices must be either macOS devices that are registered and managed or Windows devices that are registered (but don't need to be managed).

options.text: A Windows device that is registered
options.explanation: Correct because to be enrolled in Okta Verify targeted devices must be either macOS devices that are registered and managed or Windows devices that are registered (but don't need to be managed).

options.text: A macOS device that is registered and managed
options.explanation: Correct because to be enrolled in Okta Verify targeted devices must be either macOS devices that are registered and managed or Windows devices that are registered (but don't need to be managed).

stem: Is this a task that the Rate Limit Dashboard allows an Okta Administrator to do?

options.text: Block specific IPs from utilizing the Okta API
options.explanation: Incorrect because the Rate Limit Dashboard does NOT allow an Okta Administrator to block specific IPs from utilizing the Okta API

options.text: Help understand the use of each API and investigate potential rate limit issues
options.explanation: Correct because the Rate Limit Dashboard allows an adminstrator to see an overview of all API rate limit events over a period of up to 3 months

options.text: Enforce Client-based rate limiting
options.explanation: Correct because the Rate Limit Dashboard allows administrators to set Client-based rate limiting on the Okta hosted login page and the Authentication API

options.text: Manage existing API Tokens
options.explanation: Incorrect because the Rate Limit Dashboard does NOT allow an Okta Administrator to manage existing API Tokens

stem: Is this statement true about app integration notes?

options.text: App integration notes can reduce help desk calls, provide troubleshooting assistance, and increase end-user self-service
App integration notes will be displayed when a user hovers over the app card on the Okta End-User Dashboard

stem: An Okta administrator discovers that some functions are NOT working as expected after creating an API token. Is this a possible cause?

options.text: The token was created with Super Administrator permissions
options.explanation: Incorrect because the operations that can be performed by the API token are determined by the specific privileges of the Okta Administrator who created the token. Super Administrator permissions would allow all API functions.

options.text: The Okta Administrator does NOT have correct permissions
options.explanation: Incorrect because the operations that can be performed by the API token are determined by the specific privileges of the Okta Administrator who created the token

stem:
Workday is the profile source for Okta and Okta provisions users to Active Directory. The usernames from Workday have this format: user@abc.com. An Okta Administrator is tasked with provisioning users to AD with a different User Principal Name (UPN) of user@xyz.com.
Is this the best way the administrator can accomplish the task?

options.text: Use attribute level mastering to assign UPN from AD
options.explanation: Incorrect because attribute level mastering will only import from AD, it will not update attributes in AD.

options.text: Use attribute mapping and Okta Expression Language to change the domain for the UPN in Profile Editor
options.explanation: Correct because Okta Expression Language allows transformation of attribute values.

options.text: Modify the profile source priority to make AD the primary profile source and Workday the secondary profile source
options.explanation: Incorrect because profile source priority will only import data from profile sources, it will not update attributes in the profile sources.

stem: Is this the correct username format when testing Delegated Authentication against Active Directory?

options.text: UPN - Universal Principal Name
options.explanation: Correct as Okta requires using the UPN when testing Delegated Authentication against Active Directory.

options.text: Display Name
options.explanation: Incorrect as Okta does NOT require using Display Name when testing Delegated Authentication against Active Directory.

stem: Is this a benefit of using SAML for authentication and authorization?

options.text: SAML improves the customer sign-in experience
options.explanation: Correct because SAML helps reduce the attack surface for organizations and improves the customer's sign-in experience.

options.text: SAML enables authentication for applications that do NOT support federated Single Sign-On (SSO)
options.explanation: Incorrect because SAML supports federated SSO.

stem: Is this a true statement about Okta Workflows?

options.text: A card represents one specific step in a flow.
options.explanation: Correct because individual cards are used to build a Workflow and represent specific steps in a flow.

options.text: Application access for Workflows is controlled by either API tokens or Node.js commands.
options.explanation: Incorrect because application access for Workflows is controlled by either API tokens or OAuth scopes.

options.text: Cards are hard coded and NOT customizable.
options.explanation: Incorrect because cards are NOT hard coded and are very customizable. An Okta Administrator can change the title and description of a card, reorder the fields that are included on a card, or select the output fields to be displayed on the card.

options.text: An Okta Administrator can check the history logs of a flow for the past 180 days.
options.explanation: Incorrect because an Okta Administrator can check flow history logs for all the times a flow has run in the past 30 days.

options.text: The use of any Event or Action card in a Flow requires a connection with a third-party application
options.explanation: Correct beccause Okta Workflows requires connectino to a third party application either through a pre-built Okta Connector or via a custom API connection using the API Connector card

stem: Is this capability supported by the Okta org authorization server but NOT supported by the Okta custom authorization server?

options.text: Mint Access Tokens with Okta API Scopes
options.explanation: Correct because this capability is supported by the Okta org authorization server but NOT the Okta custom authorization server.

stem: Is this a supported feature in Okta Org2Org?

options.text: Authentication through WS-Federation
options.explanation: Incorrect because Okta Org2Org only supports SAML or SWA for authentication.

options.text: Import profile updates
options.explanation: Correct because updates made to a user's profile in the connected org (spoke) are downloaded and applied to the Okta user profile in the central Okta org (hub).

options.text: Automate password reset
options.explanation: Incorrect because this is an Okta Automation option but NOT a feature of Org2Org.

options.text: Authentication through OIDC
options.explanation: Incorrect because Okta Org2Org only supports SAML or SWA for authentication.

options.text: Import New Users
options.explanation: Correct because users created in the connected org (spoke) can be imported into the central Okta org (hub).

stem:
An Okta Administrator is configuring a global session policy rule.
Is this a condition that can be configured to prompt users for Multifactor Authentication?

options.text: When signing in with a new device cookie
options.explanation: Correct because it is one of the available options to prompt users for Multifactor Authentication.

options.text: When the user has NOT signed on in the past 14 days
options.explanation: Incorrect because an Okta Administrator CANNOT select this option to prompt users for Multifactor Authentication.

stem: Is this a valid use case for utilizing a Routing Rule with an external Identity Provider (IdP)?

options.text: A routing rule can route to alternate or legacy authentication for off-network users and use Okta for on-network users
options.explanation: Correct as a routing rule can use a user's network location as criteria.

options.text: A routing rule can block specific users from accessing an external IdP
options.explanation: Incorrect as a routing rule cannot block users from accessing an external IdP.

stem: Can an Okta Administrator use this parameter to define a dynamic zone?

options.text: Autonomous System Number (ASN)
IP address type
Location

stem:
An Okta Administrator needs to ensure high availability of the organization's Okta On Prem Provisioning (OPP) agent.
Will this configuration achieve the administrator's objective?

options.text: Modify the OktaAgentService.exe.instance file and configure it for high availability
options.explanation: Incorrect because the Okta On Prem Provisioning (OPP) agent intelligently configures high availability when two or more Okta OPP agents are installed on separate servers. In addition, the OktaAgentService.exe.instance file is not used for the OPP agent configuration.

options.text: Install and configure at least two OPP server agents on separate servers
options.explanation: Correct because the Okta On Prem Provisioning (OPP) agent intelligently configures high availability when two or more Okta OPP agents are installed on separate servers.

stem: Is this something an Okta Administrator should consider when using the LDAP agent for integrating an LDAP directory with Okta?

options.text: Push groups should be used for provisioning Okta groups to the LDAP directory
options.explanation: Incorrect as Push Groups are not supported by the LDAP agent.

stem: Is this the expected behavior when an incremental import from an integration is performed?

options.text: Only updated users since the last import are brought into Okta
options.explanation: Incorrect because updated users and groups are imported in an incremental import.

options.text: Updated users and groups since the last import are brought into Okta

stem:
An Okta Administrator is configuring an Okta Radius agent server for VPN service. While testing, the administrator noticed the authentication is failing. The authentication logs show an issue with a malformed username, even though the user has entered it correctly.
Is this a possible cause for the issue?

options.text: TCP Port 443 is NOT open between the Radius agent server and Okta services.
options.explanation: Incorrect because if the administrator can see a malformed username in the logs, it means there is communication between the Okta Radius agent and Okta services.

options.text: The VPN server is using MSCHAPv2 instead of PAP authentication.
options.explanation: Correct because a malformed username in the logs indicates that the VPN server is using MSCHAPv2 to encode the username. The administrator can fix the issue by making sure that only PAP authentication is enabled on the VPN server.

options.text: UDP Port 1812 is not opened between the Radius agent server and the VPN gateway.
options.explanation: Incorrect because if the administrator can see a malformed username in the logs, it means there is communication between the Okta Radius agent and the VPN server.

stem: A company is considering Okta Workflows to replace their legacy code for onboarding and off-boarding of users. Can this scenario be automated with Okta Workflows?

options.text: Configure a direct, real-time sync with an application.
options.explanation: Incorrect because Okta Workflows does NOT support synchronous flows.

options.text: Trigger onboarding when a user is created in Okta
Provision or deprovision users in downstream applications
Run scheduled workflows to audit or clean up users
Replace real-time authentication or inline authorization logic

stem: Is this the default period of time that Okta stores events in the System Log?

options.text: 30 days
60 days
365 days
90 days

stem: Can an Okta Administrator configure this as a profile source?

options.text: Profile enrollment policy
options.explanation: Incorrect as the profile enrollment policy determines what data is required from a user before they can access an app. It is not related to profile sourcing.

options.text: Google Workspace
options.explanation: Correct because Google Workspace is an app that allows profile sourcing.

stem: Is this how an Okta Administrator can delete a pre-existing group in a downstream application via Group Push?

options.text: Select the Push Now option
Rename the group
Link an Okta Group to the pre-existing downstream group and then unlink the push group selecting "Delete the group in the target app"
Link an Okta Group to the pre-existing downstream group and then delete the Okta group
Select "Delete the group in the target app"

stem:
An Okta Administrator is trying to find events in which the user was allowed access and the sign-in request was evaluated as suspicious by ThreatInsight.
Is this a valid query the administrator can use to filter the System Log to find these events?

options.text: eventType eq "security.threat.detected" or debugContext.debugData.th eq "true"
options.explanation: Incorrect because it does not include a search query for end user allowed access. debugContext.debugData.th eq "true" is another way of filtering ThreatInsight's flagged suspicious events.

options.text: eventType eq "user.session.start" or debugContext.debugData.th eq "true"

stem:
In an Okta org, there are two profile sources: Active Directory (AD) and Workday. Workday must be the profile source for Title.
Is this the correct action for the Okta Administrator to take?

options.text: On the Okta user profile, override the Title attribute from the Workday profile source.
options.explanation: Correct because this configuration will make Workday the profile source for Title.

options.text: Make Workday the highest directory profile source in Okta.
options.explanation: Incorrect because this configuration will make Workday the profile source each time the attribute mappings between sources overlap.

stem: Does this component handle the Kerberos validation in Agentless Desktop Single Sign-On (DSSO)?

options.text: Active Directory (AD) agent
options.explanation: Incorrect because the AD agent is used for integrating Okta with Active Directory.

options.text: Okta service
options.explanation: Correct because with agentless DSSO, Kerberos validation is done in the cloud in Okta.

options.text: Internet Information Services (IIS) server
options.explanation: Incorrect because the IIS server handles Kerberos validation in agent-based DSSO.

stem:
An Okta Super Administrator is tasked with configuring delegated authentication from Okta to Active Directory (AD). The Administrator needs to adhere to the principle of least privilege.
Is this a true statement about the Okta service account in this situation?

options.text: The Okta service account must be able to read the Organizational Units (OU) and the user objects for the users who will be authenticating against Okta.
options.explanation: Correct because the Okta service account must be able to discover and read the User objects it will be providing delegated authentication to.

options.text: The Okta service account does NOT require any special AD permissions in order to facilitate delegated authentication.
options.explanation: Correct because the Okta service account does not require any special AD permissions in order to authenticate the users.

options.text: The Okta service account must have the ability to set user password and force apssword change on next logon.
options.explanation: Incorrect because the Okta service account does NOT need permissions to reset user passwords in order to authenticate users.

stem: Will this action deactivate a user's downstream application account?

options.text: The user's Okta account is created.
options.explanation: Incorrect because there is no effect on a user's external application account if the user's Okta account is created.

options.text: The user's Okta account is updated
options.explanation: Incorrect because there is no effect on a user's external application account if the user's Okta account is updated.

options.text: The user's Okta account is deactivated
options.explanation: Correct because if the user's Okta account is deactivated, the user will be unassigned from the application and the user's external application account will be automatically deactivated.

options.text: The user is unassigned from the app integration in Okta.
options.explanation: Correct because if the user's Okta account is deactivated, the user will be unassigned from the application and the user's external application account will be automatically deactivated.
